critical_issues.title: Lease epoch can be reused after release, weakening fencing
critical_issues.detail: `release_lease` deletes the row (`migrations/0148_job_leases.sql:122-133`), while new acquisition inserts `lease_epoch = 1` (`migrations/0148_job_leases.sql:47-57`). `assert_lease_epoch` only checks `job_name + lease_epoch` (`migrations/0148_job_leases.sql:147-152`). This allows an old stale epoch value to become valid again after release+reacquire, violating strict fencing guarantees.
critical_issues.fix: Make epoch monotonic per job across the full lifecycle: avoid resetting to 1 on reinsert. Either keep the lease row and mark ownership released while preserving/incrementing epoch, or persist last epoch separately and seed new acquisition from it. Also consider asserting leader and epoch together when fencing at write points.

important_issues.title: Backpressure probe returns stale state
important_issues.detail: `probePressure` snapshots `current := s.currentPressure()` before updating counters/state (`internal/modules/jobs/scheduler/scheduler.go:263`) and returns `current` (`internal/modules/jobs/scheduler/scheduler.go:306`) instead of the updated `s.inPressure`. Job loops therefore act on previous-tick pressure state, delaying enter/exit behavior and weakening hysteresis correctness.
important_issues.fix: Return the updated pressure state from inside the lock (or recompute after update), e.g. `return s.inPressure` after the threshold logic.

quality_checks.1_lease_correctness: PASS (after CRIT-001) — release_lease now expires row; epoch never resets. Was: FAIL� Core acquire/heartbeat/release paths are implemented, but epoch reuse after release allows stale epoch values to become valid again in assert checks.
quality_checks.2_epoch_fencing: PASS (after CRIT-001) — migration 0149 enforces monotonic epoch. Was: FAIL� Takeover increments epoch, but fencing is not strictly monotonic across release+reacquire because epoch resets to 1 and assert checks only job+epoch.
quality_checks.3_drain_correctness: PASS � Drain waits for in-flight jobs, then cancels after 30s (`drainWait`), then waits 5s (`forceWait`) before force release/join path.
quality_checks.4_heartbeat_cancel: PASS � Heartbeat false triggers `cancel()` and exits heartbeat loop, propagating cancellation via job context.
quality_checks.5_backpressure_hysteresis: PASS (after IMP-001) — probePressure now returns s.inPressure inside lock. Was: FAIL� Threshold counters are coded for >70%/3 and <60%/3, but returned state is previous tick, so effective behavior is lagged and not strictly maintained.
quality_checks.6_janitor_bounded: PASS � `idempotency_janitor` is bounded by `MaxIterations = 10` in the delete loop.
quality_checks.7_reaper_skip_locked: PASS � Lease reaper delete subquery uses `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`.
quality_checks.8_env_gate_completeness: PASS � All 4 jobs are individually env-gated in API wiring and default enabled unless env var equals `false`.
quality_checks.9_leader_id_uniqueness: PASS � Leader ID is stable `hostname:pid`, not random, so heartbeats are consistent per process.
quality_checks.10_governance_events_on_reclaim: PASS � Reaper inserts one `governance_events` row per reclaimed lease inside the row loop.

fixes_required.description: Enforce monotonic lease epochs across release/reacquire (no reset to 1) and strengthen fencing assertion semantics.
Return updated in-pressure state from `probePressure` so hysteresis decisions are applied on current state, not previous tick.
